food_name: Cooked Flat Rice Noodle / Kway Teow (plain)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a rice noodle concept but no specific flat rice noodle (kway teow) concept. Nearest parent is a rice-based noodle product. Recommend new concept.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pasta and noodles — flat rice noodle, cooked (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of flat rice noodles for a hawker dish weighs approximately 200g (cooked) and forms the noodle base for dishes such as Char Kway Teow and Pad See Ew.

nutrient_source.title: Flat Rice Noodles (Kway Teow) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database; values adapted from HPB fresh rice noodle data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact standalone kway teow entry found in HPB Foodo. Values derived from HPB rice noodle product data and USDA rice noodle composition.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — rice noodles, freshly made (cooked); GI moderate-high due to rice flour starch gelatinisation.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 10mg per serving (200g) of plain noodles — essentially sodium-free; all sodium in kway teow dishes comes from sauces and seasonings.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should manage sodium through dish sauces rather than the kway teow noodles, which contain negligible sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~61) from gelatinised rice flour; per-serving glycaemic load approximately 32; slightly higher GI than yellow egg noodles due to absence of egg.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should be aware that flat rice noodles have a moderately higher GI than yellow egg noodles or pasta; portion control to 150–200g cooked is advisable.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nutritionally neutral for inflammation; gluten-free and allergen-free; provides clean energy without inflammatory compounds.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Flat rice noodles are suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis, particularly those following a gluten-free diet; they provide clean, easily digestible carbohydrate energy without pro-inflammatory additives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flat rice noodles (kway teow) are wide, flat, silky white noodles made from rice flour and water, sold fresh in Singapore's hawker centres and wet markets. They have a soft, slippery texture and a mild, clean rice flavour that absorbs sauces and wok char exceptionally well, making them the preferred base for Char Kway Teow. Fresh kway teow are entirely allergen-free (no wheat, no egg), gluten-free, and vegan.
common_user_queries: how many calories in kway teow
kway teow vs bee hoon which has fewer calories
flat rice noodles GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No standalone kway teow entry found in HPB Foodo. Values derived from HPB rice noodle product data and USDA cross-reference. Confidence medium.
notes: Vegan, gluten-free, and allergen-free. Nutritionally simple — primarily starch and water. The low protein content means kway teow must be paired with protein-rich toppings for nutritional completeness. Fresh kway teow has a shorter shelf life than dried rice noodles.
